title: 11 Herbal Creams For Tinnitus
intro_desc: Herbal creams for tinnitus are a natural way to help manage the symptoms of this condition. Tinnitus is a ringing or buzzing in the ears, and it can be really frustrating. But some herbal teas have been shown to help relieve tinnitus. These herbs work by reducing inflammation in the body and improving blood flow to the ears.  For example, Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, has been used for centuries to calm the mind and body. It's a natural anti-inflammatory, which means it can help reduce the swelling in the ears that can cause tinnitus. Ginkgo biloba, on the other hand, is known for its ability to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation. This can help to reduce the ringing in the ears and improve hearing. Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian root, is a natural sedative that can help to calm the nervous system and reduce stress.  Using herbal teas for tinnitus can bring many benefits to your life. For one, it's a natural and non-invasive way to manage your symptoms. This means you can avoid the side effects of medication and surgery. Herbal teas are also a great way to relax and reduce stress, which can help to improve your overall well-being. By using herbal creams and teas, you can take control of your tinnitus and improve your quality of life.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis creams contains boiactive constituents like linalool, linalyl acetate, and geraniol. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce stress and anxiety. The antispasmodic properties of Melissa officinalis creams can also help relax tense muscles and improve blood flow to the ears. This can be beneficial for people with tinnitus, as it may help reduce the symptoms of the condition. By reducing stress and improving blood flow, Melissa officinalis creams may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal creams for tinnitus
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of Melissa officinalis flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 tablespoons of lavender oil.
Heat coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water until beeswax melts.
Add Melissa officinalis flowers to the mixture and let it steep for 2-3 hours. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Add vitamin E oil and lavender oil to the mixture. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify. Store the creams in a cool, dry place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams contains active constituents like flavonoids, terpenoids, and bilobalide. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the ear and alleviate tinnitus symptoms. Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties also help protect the ear's delicate structures from damage caused by free radicals. The cream's vasodilating properties improve blood flow to the ears, which may help reduce tinnitus symptoms. By reducing inflammation, protecting the ear, and improving blood flow, Ginkgo biloba creams may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for tinnitus
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Ginkgo biloba extract (dried and powdered), and 2 teaspoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Once melted, add the beeswax and stir until it's fully dissolved.
Add the Ginkgo biloba extract and vitamin E oil to the melted mixture. Stir well to combine.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool until it reaches a temperature of around 120°F (49°C) to 130°F (54°C). Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis creams contains valerenic acid, isovaleric acid, and valepotriates as its bioactive constituents. These compounds have a sedative effect, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that may contribute to tinnitus. Valerenic acid also has a neuroprotective property, which can help protect the auditory nerve from damage. Additionally, the valepotriates in Valeriana officinalis creams have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the ear and surrounding areas. By reducing stress and inflammation, Valeriana officinalis creams may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal creams for tinnitus
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: Valeriana officinalis root extract (500mg), coconut oil (1 cup), beeswax (2 tablespoons), shea butter (2 tablespoons), vitamin E oil (10 drops), and a double boiler.
Melt coconut oil, beeswax, and shea butter in the double boiler over low heat. Stir until beeswax is melted.
Add Valeriana officinalis root extract to the melted mixture and stir well.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool slightly. Add vitamin E oil and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely before using. Store in a cool place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains active compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce tinnitus symptoms. These compounds work by blocking the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that can cause pain and inflammation in the inner ear. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of gingerols and shogaols can also help protect the hair cells in the ear from damage, which is a common cause of tinnitus. Furthermore, the warming effect of ginger can increase blood flow to the ears, which may help to reduce symptoms of tinnitus. The combination of these properties makes Zingiber officinale creams a potential treatment option for people suffering from tinnitus.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for tinnitus
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of distilled water, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of ginger root powder, 2 tablespoons of olive oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of boiling water.
Add distilled water, olive oil, and ginger root powder to the melted mixture. Stir well.
Remove from heat and let cool for 5-10 minutes. Stir occasionally until mixture thickens.
Strain the mixture into a clean container. Let cool and harden before use. Apply a small amount to affected ear area.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum creams contains hyperforin and hypericin as its active constituents. These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the inflammation and oxidative stress associated with tinnitus. Hyperforin, in particular, has been shown to have a protective effect on the hair cells in the inner ear, which are responsible for sound processing. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, Hypericum perforatum creams may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms by promoting a healthy environment for the hair cells to function properly. Regular use of Hypericum perforatum creams may help provide relief from tinnitus by addressing the underlying causes of the condition.
remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal creams for tinnitus
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Hypericum perforatum extract, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Once melted, remove from heat and let cool slightly. Add the Hypericum perforatum extract, shea butter, and vitamin E oil. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a bowl or container and let it cool and solidify. This may take about 30 minutes, depending on the temperature.
Once solidified, use a spatula to scoop out the desired amount and apply directly to the affected ear. Repeat as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams contains a high concentration of linalool and linalyl acetate, two powerful compounds that have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation. These active constituents can help alleviate the symptoms of tinnitus by calming the nervous system and reducing stress. The anti-inflammatory properties of linalool and linalyl acetate can also help to reduce the pressure and irritation in the ear that can contribute to tinnitus. Additionally, the calming effects of Lavandula angustifolia can help to reduce anxiety and improve sleep quality, which are often linked to tinnitus. By applying Lavandula angustifolia creams to the skin, the active constituents can be absorbed and delivered directly to the affected areas, where they can begin to work to alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for tinnitus
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, and 2 teaspoons of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers.
Heat the distilled water in a saucepan over low heat. Add the dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the infused water and discard the flowers. In a separate bowl, mix the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax mixture. Once melted, add the infused water to the mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify. Store the cream in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains boiactive constituents like alkylamides and caffeic acid, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help reduce inflammation in the ear, a common cause of tinnitus. The flavonoids and phenolic acids in Echinacea purpurea creams also have antioxidant properties, which protect the ear from damage and promote healing. The alkylamides in Echinacea purpurea creams have been shown to reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to tinnitus. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, Echinacea purpurea creams may provide relief from tinnitus symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for tinnitus
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, and 1/4 cup of beeswax.
Combine dried Echinacea flowers and coconut oil in a saucepan and heat over low heat for 2 hours, then let it cool.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl, discarding the solids.
In a separate saucepan, melt the beeswax over low heat. Add the strained Echinacea mixture and stir until combined.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify. Store in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium creams contains sesquiterpene lactones, including achilleine and alantolactone. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the ear and promote healing. The creams also contain flavonoids and polyphenols, which have been shown to improve blood circulation and reduce oxidative stress in the auditory system. The combination of these bioactive constituents can help alleviate tinnitus symptoms by reducing inflammation and promoting healthy blood flow to the ears. By applying the cream topically, people with tinnitus may experience relief from the condition.
remedies.image_alt: Achillea millefolium herbal creams for tinnitus
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Achillea millefolium flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, and 1/4 cup of shea butter.
In a saucepan, heat 1/2 cup of coconut oil over low heat.
Add 1/4 cup of shea butter to the saucepan and stir until melted.
Add 1 cup of dried Achillea millefolium flowers to the saucepan and heat for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and let it cool. Store the cream in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams contains rosmarinic acid, camphor, and borneol as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the ear, a common cause of tinnitus. The antiseptic and antibacterial properties of borneol and camphor can also help combat infections that may be contributing to tinnitus. Rosmarinus officinalis has antioxidant properties, which can help protect the delicate hair cells in the ear from damage and oxidative stress. By reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and protecting the ear's delicate structures, Rosmarinus officinalis creams may be a useful treatment option for tinnitus sufferers.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal creams for tinnitus
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of rosemary essential oil, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add beeswax to the melted mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool for 5 minutes. Then, stir in rosemary essential oil and vitamin E oil.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify. Once solid, your Rosmarinus officinalis cream is ready to use for tinnitus relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum creams contains silymarin, a complex mixture of flavonoids and lignans, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Silymarin's antioxidant properties help protect the inner ear from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to tinnitus. The flavonoids in silymarin, particularly silybin, have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling in the ear and promote healing. Silymarin also improves blood flow to the ear, which helps to reduce inflammation and promote the delivery of oxygen and nutrients to the damaged cells. By reducing inflammation, improving blood flow, and protecting against oxidative damage, silymarin in Silybum marianum creams may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Silybum marianum herbal creams for tinnitus
remedy_procedure: Gather 250g of Silybum marianum flowers, dried and crushed into a powder.
Combine 250g of the Silybum marianum powder with 500ml of coconut oil in a small saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl, pressing on the solids to extract the liquid.
Transfer the liquid to a clean container and let it cool. Store in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains the bioactive constituents curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the auditory system. The anti-inflammatory properties of curcuminoids can also help reduce damage to the hair cells in the inner ear, which are often affected in tinnitus. Additionally, curcumin has been shown to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the nerves, which can help alleviate tinnitus symptoms. By reducing inflammation and promoting healthy blood flow, Curcuma longa creams may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms and improve overall ear health.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for tinnitus
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of turmeric powder (Curcuma longa), and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Stir until melted and smooth.
Add shea butter and stir until melted and combined with the oil mixture.
Add turmeric powder and vitamin E oil to the mixture. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a small tin or container and let it cool and harden. Once set, your Curcuma longa cream is ready to use.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help reduce tinnitus symptoms, but it's not proven to prevent it from forming. Some herbal teas, like ginkgo biloba and ginger, have antioxidants that might ease ringing in the ears. However, more research is needed to fully understand their effect. Tinnitus is a complex issue, and its causes are not yet fully understood.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can be a good option for tinnitus relief, but consuming them daily may have mixed effects. Some teas, like ginger and chamomile, can help calm the mind and reduce stress, which may alleviate tinnitus symptoms. However, excessive consumption of certain herbs like ginkgo biloba can worsen tinnitus in some people.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with tinnitus relief, but the time it takes to show results varies. Some people notice improvements in a few days, while others may take a few weeks. It's possible that it might take a couple of months to see noticeable changes, but everyone's experience is different.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for tinnitus can be most effective in the evening. This is because some herbal teas, such as those containing valerian root or lavender, can help calm the mind and body, making it easier to fall asleep and get some rest.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for tinnitus, which is a ringing or noise in the ears. Certain herbs like ginkgo biloba and valerian root can affect blood pressure and heart rate, mixing with medications that also do this. This might change how medications work or increase side effects, so it's essential to be aware of this possibility.
faq_pregnant: While pregnant, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas for tinnitus. Some herbs, like ginkgo biloba and ginger, might affect the baby's development or cause bleeding. However, peppermint and chamomile teas are often considered safe, but in moderation. It's always a good idea to check the ingredients and talk to the person who prepared the tea.
faq_breastfeeding: If you're breastfeeding and want to try herbal teas for tinnitus, be cautious. Some herbs like sage and peppermint are generally considered safe, but others like chamomile and lavender might affect your baby's sleep. Always check the ingredients and follow the recommended amounts to minimize risks.
title_2: Construction workers with tinnitus find relief in 11 herbal creams
